name: South Australia (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign
the markings of the piping shrike, also in the Union Jack
the crosses of the Union Jack
the gold disc bearing the state badge and the rays behind the shrike
the body of the piping shrike

description: A blue British ensign with the Union Jack in the canton and a yellow disc in the fly bearing the state badge: a piping shrike with wings outstretched on a gum-tree branch.
history: Adopted on 14 January 1904. The badge — a white-backed magpie on a gum-tree branch with rising-sun rays behind — was designed by Robert Craig of the Adelaide School of Arts.

features.symbolism: Based on the British Blue Ensign, the standard format for Australian state flags.
the Union Jack, signifying historic ties to the United Kingdom

features.role: yellow disc bearing the state badge
features.symbolism: the gold disc serves as the field for the state badge

features.role: piping shrike (white-backed magpie) with wings outstretched, perched on a gum-tree branch
features.symbolism: the piping shrike (Cracticus tibicen), associated with South Australia
